title: loeselia glandulosa subsp. glandulosa
intro: Loeselia glandulosa subsp. glandulosa, also known as the glandular mountain-loeelia or mountain-loeelia, is a plant species belonging to the borage family (Boraginaceae) and is native to the western United States. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Loeselia glandulosa subsp. glandulosa include its potential to alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its use in wound healing and skin conditions. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties for various health benefits.
